volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 891 - Section 2.1095)

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἔγνων γὰρ τὸ προσφώνημά σου ,
sections.translation: Dearest of men ! I know your voice . Terrible are the things I have just suffered at the hands of this man here .

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα ταῦτα , τίς δʼ ὁ πημήνας ; λέγε .
sections.translation: What things are these ? And who has pained you ? Speak !

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: Κρέων ὅδʼ , ὃν δέδορκας , οἴχεται τέκνων
sections.translation: Creon , whom you see here ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ἀποσπάσας μου τὴν μόνην ξυνωρίδα .
sections.translation: has torn from me my children — my only two .

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ;
sections.translation: What is that you say ?

sections.label: Section 896a
sections.original: οἷά περ πέπονθʼ ἀκήκοας .
sections.translation: You have heard my wrongs .

sections.label: Section 897
sections.original: οὔκουν τις ὡς τάχιστα προσπόλων μολὼν
sections.translation: Hurry , one of you attendants , to the altars there , and order the people to leave the sacrifice

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: σπεύδειν ἀπὸ ῥυτῆρος , ἔνθα δίστομοι
sections.translation: and race on foot and by horse full speed , to the region where the two highways meet , so that the maidens may not pass , and I not become a mockery to this stranger as one worsted by force . Quick , I say , away with you !

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: εἰ μὲν διʼ ὀργῆς ἧκον , ἧς ὅδʼ ἄξιος ,
sections.translation: anger went as far as he deserves , I would not let him go uninjured from my hand . But now , just such law as he himself has brought will be the rule for his correction .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτʼ ἔξει τῆσδε τῆς χώρας , πρὶν ἂν
sections.translation: You will never leave this land

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κείνας ἐναργεῖς δεῦρό μοι στήσῃς ἄγων ·
sections.translation: until you bring those maidens and produce them in my sight . For your action is a disgrace to me , and to your own ancestors , and to your country . You have come to a city that practices justice and sanctions nothing without law ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: τὰ τῆσδε τῆς γῆς κύριʼ , ὧδʼ ἐπεισπεσὼν
sections.translation:
yet you have spurned her lawful authorities and made this violent assault . You are taking captives at will and subjugating them by force , as if you believed that my city was void of men , or manned by slaves , and that I counted for nothing .
Yet it was not Thebes that trained you to be evil . Thebes is not accustomed to rearing unjust men ;—

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ φιλοῦσιν ἄνδρας ἐκδίκους τρέφειν ,
sections.translation: nor would she praise you , if she learned that you are despoiling me , and despoiling the gods , when by force you drive off their unfortunate suppliants . If my foot were upon your land , never would I drag off or lead away someone

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: οὐδʼ εἰ τὰ πάντων εἶχον ἐνδικώτατα ,
sections.translation: without permission from the ruler of the land , whoever he might be — no , even if my claim were the most just of all . I would know how a stranger ought to live among citizens . But you are disgracing a city that does not deserve it : your own ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: τὴν αὐτὸς αὑτοῦ , καί σʼ ὁ πληθύων χρόνος
sections.translation:
and your years , despite their fullness , bring you an old age barren of sense .
Now , I have said before , and I say it once again : let the maidens be brought here speedily , unless you wish to be an unwilling immigrant to this country by force .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: εἶναι βίᾳ τε κοὐχ ἑκών · καὶ ταῦτά σοι
sections.translation: These are the words of my lips ; my mind is in accord .

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ἵνʼ ἥκεις , ὦ ξένʼ ; ὡς ἀφʼ ὧν μὲν εἶ
sections.translation: Do you see your plight , stranger ? You are judged to be just by where you are from , but your deeds are found to be evil .

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: ἐγὼ οὔτʼ ἄνανδρον τήνδε τὴν πόλιν νέμων ,
sections.translation: It is not because I thought this city void of men , son of Aegeus , or of counsel , as you say ,

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον Αἰγέως , οὔτʼ ἄβουλον , ὡς σὺ φῄς ,
sections.translation: that I have done this deed ; but because I judged that its people could never be so zealous for my relatives as to support them against my will . And I knew that this people would not receive a parricide and a polluted man ,

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: κἄναγνον οὐ δεξοίατʼ , οὐδʼ ὅτῳ γάμοι
sections.translation: a man whose unholy marriage — a marriage with children — had been found out . Such wisdom , I knew , was immemorial on the Areopagus , which does not allow such wanderers to dwell within this city . Trusting in that , I sought to take this prize .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ᾧ πίστιν ἴσχων τήνδʼ ἐχειρούμην ἄγραν .
sections.translation: And I would not have done so , had he not been calling down bitter curses on me and on my race . As I was wronged in this way , I judged that I had a right to this requital . For anger knows no old age , until death comes ;

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: θανεῖν · θανόντων δʼ οὐδὲν ἄλγος ἅπτεται .
sections.translation:
the dead alone feel no galling pain .
In response to this , you will do what pleases you ; for , though my case is just , the lack of aid makes me weak . Yet in the face of your actions , despite my age , I will endeavor to pay you back .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: ὦ λῆμʼ ἀναιδές , τοῦ καθυβρίζειν δοκεῖς ,
sections.translation: Shameless arrogance , where do you think this outrage falls — on my old age , or on your own ? Bloodshed , incest , misery — all this your tongue has launched against me , and all this I have borne in my wretchedness by no choice of mine .

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: τάχʼ ἄν τι μηνίουσιν εἰς γένος πάλαι .
sections.translation: For this was dear to the gods , who were angry , perhaps , with my race from of old . Taking me alone , you could not find a reproach for any crime , in retribution for which I was driven to commit these sins against myself and against my kin . Tell me now : if , by the voice of an oracle , some divine doom was coming on my father ,

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: χρησμοῖσιν ἱκνεῖθʼ ὥστε πρὸς παίδων θανεῖν ,
sections.translation: that he should die by a son ’ s hand , how could you justly reproach me with this , when I was then unborn , when no father had yet begotten me , no mother ’ s womb conceived me ? But if , having been born to misery — as I was born — I came to blows with my father and slew him , ignorant of what

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: ἐς χεῖρας ἦλθον πατρὶ καὶ κατέκτανον ,
sections.translation:
I was doing and to whom , how could you reasonably blame the unwitting deed ?
And my mother — wretch , do you feel no shame in forcing me to speak of her marriage , when she was your sister , and when it was such as I will now tell ?

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: σοῦ γʼ εἰς τόδʼ ἐξελθόντος ἀνόσιον στόμα .
sections.translation: For I will not be silent , when you have gone so far in impious speech . Yes , she was my mother , yes — alas , for my miseries ! I did not know it , nor did she , and to her shame she bore children to the son whom she had borne .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: κείνην τε ταῦτα δυσστομεῖν · ἐγὼ δέ νιν
sections.translation:
But one thing , at least , I know : that you willingly revile her and me , but I did not willingly marry her , and I do not willingly speak now .
No , I will not be called evil on account of this marriage , nor in the slaying of my father , which you charge me with again and again in bitter insult .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: φόνους πατρῴους ἐξονειδίζων πικρῶς .
sections.translation: Answer just one thing of those I ask . If , here and now , someone should come up and try to murder you — you , the just one — would you ask if the murderer was your father , or would you revenge yourself on him straightaway ?

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: δοκῶ μέν , εἴπερ ζῆν φιλεῖς , τὸν αἴτιον
sections.translation: I think that if your life is dear to you , you would requite the criminal , and not look around for a justification . Such then were the evils into which I came , led by the gods ; and in this , I think , my father ’ s soul , could it come back to life , would not contradict me .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , εἶ γὰρ οὐ δίκαιος , ἀλλʼ ἅπαν καλὸν
sections.translation: But you are not just ; you are one who considers it a fine thing to utter every sort of word , both those which are sanctioned and those which are forbidden — such are your taunts against me in the presence of these men . And to you it seems a fine thing to flatter the renowned Theseus , and Athens , saying how well it is governed .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: κᾆθʼ ὧδʼ ἐπαινῶν πολλὰ τοῦδʼ ἐκλανθάνει ,
sections.translation: Yet while giving such generous praise , you forget that if any land knows how to worship the gods with honors , this land excels in that . It is from her that you had planned to steal me , a suppliant and an old man , and tried to seize me , having already carried off my daughters .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἀνθʼ ὧν ἐγὼ νῦν τάσδε τὰς θεὰς ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Therefore I now call on the goddesses here , I supplicate them , I beseech them with prayers , to bring me help and to fight on my behalf , that you may learn well what kind of men this city is guarded by .

sections.label: Section 1014
sections.original: ὁ ξεῖνος , ὦναξ , χρηστός · αἱ δὲ συμφοραὶ
sections.translation: The stranger is a good man , lord .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: αὐτοῦ πανώλεις , ἄξιαι δʼ ἀμυναθεῖν .
sections.translation: His fate has been accursed , but it is worthy of our aid .

sections.label: Section 1016
sections.original: ἅλις λόγων , ὡς οἱ μὲν ἐξειργασμένοι
sections.translation: Enough of words . The doers of the deed are in flight , while we , the sufferers , stand still .

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἀμαυρῷ φωτὶ προστάσσεις ποεῖν ;
sections.translation: What order , then , do you have for a powerless man ?

sections.label: Section 1019
sections.original: ὁδοῦ κατάρχειν τῆς ἐκεῖ , πομπὸν δέ με
sections.translation: Guide the way on the path to them while I escort you ,

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: χωρεῖν , ἵνʼ , εἰ μὲν ἐν τόποισι τοῖσδʼ ἔχεις
sections.translation: in order that if you are keeping the maidens whom we seek in these lands , you yourself may reveal them to me . But if your men are fleeing with the spoils in their grasp , we may spare our trouble ; the chase is for others , from whom they will never escape out of this land to thank their gods .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξυφηγοῦ · γνῶθι δʼ ὡς ἔχων ἔχει
sections.translation: Come , lead the way ! And know that the captor has been captured ; fate has seized you as you hunted . Gains unjustly got by guile are soon lost . And you will have no ally in your purpose ; for I well know that it is not without accomplice or resource that you have come to such

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ἥκοντα τόλμης τῆς παρεστώσης τανῦν ,
sections.translation: outrage , from the daring mood which has inspired you here . There was someone you were trusting in when you did these deeds . This I must consider , and I must not make this city weaker than one man . Do you take my drift ?

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: δοκεῖ λελέχθαι χὤτε ταῦτʼ ἐμηχανῶ ;
sections.translation: Or do these words seem as empty as the warnings given when you were laying your plans ?

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: οὐδὲν σὺ μεμπτὸν ἐνθάδʼ ὢν ἐρεῖς ἐμοί ·
sections.translation: Say what you wish while you are here ; I will not object . But at home I too will know how to act .

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: χωρῶν ἀπείλει νῦν · σὺ δʼ ἡμίν , Οἰδίπους ,
sections.translation: Make your threats , then , but go forward . As for you , Oedipus , stay here in peace with my pledge that , unless I die beforehand ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἢν μὴ θάνω ʼγὼ πρόσθεν , οὐχὶ παύσομαι
sections.translation: I will not cease until I put you in possession of your children .

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: ὄναιο , Θησεῦ , τοῦ τε γενναίου χάριν
sections.translation: Thanks to you , Theseus , for your nobleness and your righteous care for me ! Theseus exits with attendants and Creon .

sections.label: Section 1.1044
sections.original: εἴην ὅθι δαΐων
sections.translation: Oh , to be where the enemy , turned to fight ,

sections.label: Section 1.1045
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν τάχʼ ἐπιστροφαὶ
sections.translation: will soon join in Ares ’ clash of bronze , by the shores of Apollo , perhaps , or by that torch-lit beach

sections.label: Section 1.1050
sections.original: οὗ πότνιαι σεμνὰ τιθηνοῦνται τέλη
sections.translation: where the Great Goddesses maintain awful rites for mortals on whose lips the ministering Eumolpidae have laid the golden seal of silence . There , I think , the war-rousing

sections.label: Section 1.1055
sections.original: Θησέα καὶ τὰς διστόλους
sections.translation: Theseus and the two maiden sisters will soon meet within our borders , amid the war-cry of resisting men !

sections.label: Section 1.1059
sections.original: ἤ που τὸν ἐφεσπέρου
sections.translation: Or perhaps they will soon draw near to the pastures on the west of Oea ’ s snowy rock ,

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: πέτρας νιφάδος πελῶσʼ
sections.translation: fleeing on young horses or in chariots racing full speed . He will be caught !

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: ἁλώσεται · δεινὸς ὁ προσχώρων Ἄρης ,
sections.translation: Terrible is the neighboring Ares , terrible the might of the followers of Theseus . Yes , the steel of every bridle flashes ,

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: ἄμβασις , οἳ τὰν ἱππίαν
sections.translation: and against their opponents charges forward our whole cavalry , who honor horse-riding Athena , and the earth-girdling Sea-god , the dear son of Rhea .

sections.label: Section 2.1074
sections.original: ἔρδουσʼ ἢ μέλλουσιν ; ὡς
sections.translation: Is the battle now or yet to be ?

sections.label: Section 2.1075
sections.original: προμνᾶταί τί μοι
sections.translation:
For somehow my mind presages to me that soon I will meet the maidens who have suffered fearfully , who have found fearful suffering at the hands of a kinsman .
Today Zeus will bring something to completion .

sections.label: Section 2.1080
sections.original: αἰθερίας νεφέλας κύρσαιμʼ ἄνωθʼ ἀγώνων
sections.translation: I predict noble struggles . Oh , to be a dove with the strength and swiftness of a whirlwind , that I might reach an airy cloud , and hang my gaze above the fight !

sections.label: Section 2.1085
sections.original: ἰὼ θεῶν πάνταρχε , παντ-
sections.translation: Hear , all-ruling lord of the gods , all-seeing Zeus ! Grant to the guardians of this land to achieve with triumphant might the capture that gives the prize into their hands ! And may your daughter grant it too , dread Pallas Athena !

sections.label: Section 2.1090
sections.original: σεμνά τε παῖς Παλλὰς Ἀθάνα .
sections.translation: And Apollo , the hunter , and his sister , who follows the spotted , swift-footed deer — I wish that they would come , a double help

sections.label: Section 2.1095
sections.original: ὦ ξεῖνʼ ἀλῆτα , τῷ σκοπῷ μὲν οὐκ ἐρεῖς
sections.translation: to this land and to its people .
